entry.content: फ़ोनपे रिफ़ंड से जुड़ी जानकारी के लिए, फ़ोनपे के ग्राहक सहायता केंद्र पर 0900-7796589या 022-858727374 पर कॉल करें. फ़ोनपे की शिकायत नीति के मुताबिक, शिकायत मिलने के 24 घंटे के अंदर जवाब दिया जाता हफ़ोनपे रिफ़ंड से जुड़ी जानकारी के लिए, फ़ोनपे के ग्राहक सहायता केंद्र पर 0900-7796589या 022-858727374 पर कॉल करें. फ़ोनपे की शिकायत नीति के मुताबिक, शिकायत मिलने के 24 घंटे के अंदर जवाब दिया जाता ह

entry.title: Re: Why db hints doesn't work?
entry.content: There's no evidence in your question that ABAP should consider your hint. Why do you think it should consider it? Are you just expecting from FAE that it would always improve the performance. That's wrong, you can't know. By default, consider that a join is better. FAE may be really slow sometimes, especially for big volumes. You should always analyze what's best for performance.

entry.title: Re: Text alignment problems with text widgets (Update?)
entry.content: Hi Alexander, It’s possible that a recent update may have changed the spike text alignment behavior in the text widgets of SAP Analytics Cloud. I recommend checking the widget’s alignment settings to see if any new options were introduced. You can also review the release notes or updates section for recent changes that could have impacted the layout. If the issue persists, it might be worth reaching out to SAP support or checking the community for others experiencing similar problems. Hope this helps!

entry.title: Re: Why db hints doesn't work?
entry.content:
Hi ,

You can run an EXPLAIN on your query:

sql
Copy code
EXPLAIN SELECT * FROM makt
APPENDING CORRESPONDING FIELDS OF TABLE lt_tab2
FOR ALL ENTRIES IN lt_tab
WHERE matnr = lt_tab-matnr
AND spras = 'E'
%_hints db2 '&max_in_blocking_factor 100&'.


This will provide you with details on how DB2 plans to execute your query, allowing you to see if your hint is being utilized.

Regards,

entry.title: Re: How do we create a variable on WEBI that calculates if the patients do the same labs within 60 d
entry.content:
To create a variable in Web rope Intelligence (WebI) that calculates whether patients did the same labs within 60 days, follow these steps:

Step 1: Create a Variable to Calculate the Difference in Days

  1. Create a variable to calculate the number of days between two lab tests.
    • Use the DaysBetween function.
    • Example formula:
      plaintext
      Copy code
      =DaysBetween([Lab Performed DateTime]; Previous([Lab Performed DateTime]))
    • This will calculate the number of days between the current lab test and the previous one for each patient.

Step 2: Filter the Labs Within 60 Days

  1. Create another variable to flag the lab tests that occurred within 60 days.
    • Example formula:
      plaintext
      Copy code
      =If([Days Between] <= 60) Then "Within 60 Days" Else "More than 60 Days"

Step 3: Calculate the Number of Patients

  1. Create a variable to count the number of patients  who did labs within 60 days.
    • Use the Count function.
    • Example:
      plaintext
      Copy code
      =Count([Patient ID] Where ([Days Between] <= 60))

This should give you the number of patients who performed the same lab test within 60 days. You can adjust this further based on your data structure. Let me know if you need more details !

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
Hi, David 

As I am a woman and recent graduate with a strong foundation in information systems, I am eager to contribute my skills to the field of SAP consulting. I am particularly interested in SAP ERP.

I am driven by a passion for technology and a desire to make a positive impact. The opportunity to gain hands-on experience and learn from industry experts through this opportunity aligns perfectly with my career goals.

entry.title: Re: What Are You Looking Forward to Learning or Doing at Devtoberfest This Year?
entry.content:
Devtoberfest sounds like an exciting event! 

*What I'm looking forward to learning or doing at Devtoberfest:*

 

1. Networking with fellow developers and industry experts.

 

2. Learning about the latest advancements in SAP development, particularly in areas like SAP Cloud, S/4HANA, and AI/ML integration.

 

3. Attending workshops and sessions on best practices for SAP development, testing, and deployment.

 

4. Exploring innovative solutions and tools in the SAP ecosystem.

 

5. Collaborating with others on coding challenges or hackathons.

*Top reason for attending Devtoberfest:*

 

1. To expand my knowledge and skills in SAP development and stay up-to-date with industry trends.

 

2. To connect with potential clients, partners, or collaborators for future projects.

 

3. To showcase my own projects or expertise through speaking or demo sessions.

 

4. To learn from industry leaders and experts in SAP development.

 

5. To be inspired by innovative ideas and solutions.

 

Some potential questions to ask at Devtoberfest:

 

- What are the most significant challenges facing SAP developers today?

 

- How can we leverage SAP Cloud and S/4HANA for more efficient development?

 

- What are the best practices for integrating AI/ML into SAP solutions?

 

- How can we ensure security and compliance in SAP development?

- What are the emerging trends and technologies in the SAP ecosystem?

entry.title: SAP SuccessFactors Learning: What’s New in the 2H 2024 Release
entry.content:
The SAP SuccessFactors 2H 2024 release has introduced a range of new features and improvements to elevate the learning experience. Here’s an overview of the highlights you need to know. 

Note: Some upgrades are currently under investigation by the SAP Learning Engineering team.

1. Approvals for Learning Requests: A Streamlined Experience

Managers, instructors, and HRBPs now have a streamlined way to approve or decline learning requests directly from the new Learning home page. This feature lets approvers manage internal and external training requests, user account requests, and course completions (with or without e-signatures) in one place. With the “Requests for Me” section, you can prioritize and manage approvals seamlessly, ensuring that important requests are promptly addressed.

 

2. Automatic Import of Open Content Network (OCN) Items

To reduce the administrative burden, SAP SuccessFactors Learning now supports automatic import of learning content from OCN providers. With a few configuration changes, admins can set up a process to import new OCN courses automatically, keeping content fresh and eliminating the need for manual updates. This is a great addition for organizations that rely on OCN providers for diverse learning content.

 

 

3. Enhanced Library Browsing with the Browse Library Feature

SAP has enhanced search functionality by adding the Browse Library feature. Learners can now filter library search results by categories, making it easier to find relevant learning activities. This update simplifies navigation and helps learners quickly access the resources they need to enhance their skills.

Note: While testing on my instance, this isnt working. SAP has marked as known issue and is under investigation by SAP engineering team.

Error I see : 

Looks like something went wrong.

 

4. Creating Custom Pages on the New Learning Home Page

You can now create custom pages on the Learning home page, tailored to specific groups within your organization. These pages can include up to five banners and eight cards per section. With this feature, you can customize learning content, create a unique experience for different user groups, and help learners easily find relevant learning activities.

Note: This took a while to reflect on home page.

 

6. Disable Option For The New Learning Experience Removed

SAP has removed the option to disable to New Learning Experience to progress towards the deprecation of the legacy experience in a future release.

 

7. New Team View for SuccessFactors Learning

If you’re using the new Learning home page, you can now access the updated Team View page to manage and review learning assignments for your team. To open the Team View page, simply go to the Dashboards menu on the Learning home page and select Team View. Your team members will appear in a list on the left side of the page.

By selecting a team member, you’ll see their detailed learning information in the right-hand detail view. If you have permissions to view team members as an alternate manager or HR business partner, a drop-down menu will appear next to the My Team header, allowing you to choose the appropriate role. Team members are listed alphabetically, with those who have overdue items shown first. The More Actions menu offers the following options for managing your team members:

  • Manage Assignments
  • Add to Learning History
  • Manage Enrollment

You can also explore individual learning details for each team member and take specific actions as needed.

 

8. Use AI to Associate Skills With Open Content Network Items

Its AI everywhere, you can use the AI-assisted capabilities to associate skills with Open Content Network (OCN) items based on the predefined skills provided by the OCN providers.

 

9. Learning History Page Updates

Based on user feedback, SAP SuccessFactors has made the Learning History page cleaner by removing columns that weren’t widely used, such as Type and Ratings. Credit hours are now available only in the Completed Activity Details section, making the page more focused and easier to navigate.

10. The New Learning Home Page Now Supports Delegation Tasks

The delegate user can now switch their role temporarily to act as their manager directly on the new Learning home page. When managers or supervisors (delegator) assign another user (delegate) to act on their behalf under certain requirements.

 

Final Thoughts

The 2H 2024 release of SAP SuccessFactors Learning introduces valuable enhancements in automation, streamlined processes, and an improved experience for both administrators and learners. With these updates, organizations can provide more tailored and secure learning environments while reducing the administrative workload. Explore these new features to elevate your organization’s learning experience today!
